title: hippocrepis comosa subsp. squamata
intro: Hippocrepis comosa subsp. squamata, also known as the squamate hairy hog's fennel, is a plant species within the legume family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects. The squamate hairy hog's fennel has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and digestive issues, due to its ability to improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and promote urination. Hippocrepis comosa subsp. squamata is typically prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits.
